plainEnglish: At Level AAA, provide context-sensitive help—inline hints, a help link near complex fields, examples, or access to support. People who get stuck on a form or task should be able to get guidance without leaving the page.
whyItMatters: Users with cognitive disabilities may be unable to complete a complex form or task without context-sensitive help available right where they're working.
quickCheck: Confirm complex forms and tasks offer context-sensitive help—inline hints, examples, or a help link near the relevant fields.
commonMistakes: Complex forms with no inline help, examples, or hints.
Burying help on a separate page far from where it's needed.
Offering no way to reach human support when self-help isn't enough.

codeExamples.label: Inline help next to a complex field
codeExamples.code: Find this on line 2 of your W-9. Need help?

codeExamples.label: Complex field with no help available
